 {"id":"ft-ombfl.4","title":"[ft-ombfl][gpu-fixture] Basic ASCII text fixture: simple paragraph + line wrap + scrollback","description":"# GPU fixture — basic ASCII text

## Background
First fixture beyond the smoketest. Validates that the harness can
correctly render normal terminal output: ASCII text, line wrapping,
scrollback.

## Why this matters
- Establishes the fixture-authoring pattern that subsequent fixtures
  follow.
- Catches a class of regressions (off-by-one in wrap point, baseline
  shift, line-spacing) that affect every other fixture.

## Scope
1. Author tests/golden/gpu/text-basic-paragraph/:
   - input.json: terminal grid 80×24 with a 5-line paragraph
     of mixed ASCII (no fancy unicode), one line that exceeds 80 cols
     to test wrap, an empty line, and a final line of dashes.
   - meta.json: viewport 80×24, dpi 1.0, tolerance defaults.
   - expected.json: list of glyph regions (optional sanity check).
   - golden.png: the rendered frame (generated by --update-goldens
     in a controlled environment, then committed).
2. Author tests/golden/gpu/text-scrollback/:
   - input.json: 80×24 viewport but 100 lines of content (scrollback).
   - meta.json: scroll position to bottom.
   - golden.png: shows the bottom-of-scrollback view.

## Out of scope
- Unicode / CJK / emoji (separate fixture).
- Interactive scrolling animation (this is static rendering).

## Approach
1. Write the fixture inputs by hand (small JSON files).
2. Run the harness with --update-goldens to generate golden.png in
   a dev-laptop controlled environment.
3. Manually inspect goldens for sanity (open in image viewer).
4. Commit fixtures + goldens.
5. Verify CI passes (after ci-wiring lands).

## Acceptance criteria
- Both fixtures (text-basic-paragraph, text-scrollback) committed.
- Each runs to PASS in `cargo test --test gpu_regression`.
- Each emits the standard JSON-line log events.
- Manual inspection confirms goldens are visually correct.

## Testing
- Fixture self-test: `cargo test --test gpu_regression text` runs
  only these two fixtures and passes.
- Mutation test: change cell content in input.json by one char;
  confirm fixture fails with a diff PNG that visually shows the
  changed cell.
- Logging verification: capture JSON-line output for both fixtures;
  assert one fixture event per fixture.

## Risks / considerations
- Don't include line-trailing whitespace differences — the comparator
  must already be tolerant or the fixture must avoid them.
- Pin a deterministic, available font for the basic tests (e.g.,
  the Liberation Mono shipped under tests/fixtures/fonts/).

## Labels
- postswarm-2026-04-27
- gpu-harness
- fixture

## References
- GPU_SCAFFOLD, GPU_RENDERER (prerequisites).
- ft-ombfl (parent).","notes":"## Polish pass 2026-04-27 (more text cases)

### Additional fixtures to author
Beyond the 2 listed in the description:
3. **text-tabs/**: a paragraph with TAB characters at column 8, 16, 24
   to verify tab-stop alignment is correct. Common renderer bug
   class: tabs interpreted as spaces or as fixed-width.
4. **text-long-line-no-break/**: an 800-char unbreakable string (no
   spaces) to verify line wrap handles non-breakable correctly.
5. **text-trailing-whitespace/**: lines with trailing spaces. Many
   terminals render selection-trailing-space differently from
   no-trailing-space. Pin the chosen behavior.
6. **text-empty-cells/**: a grid with intentionally-empty cells
   between content (e.g., bash prompt + cursor + spaces + content).
   Catches "background fill" regressions.

### Rationale
text-basic-paragraph and text-scrollback alone don't cover
tab-stop, long-line wrap, or trailing-whitespace edge cases. These 4
additional fixtures take ~20 min total to author and catch concrete
regression classes that have hit other terminals.

### Total fixtures in this bead
6 (was 2). Per-fixture work is small; total still <60 min.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod_2","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:06:19.762519Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T04:49:25.880923Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T04:49:25.879220Z","close_reason":"basic ASCII paragraph fixture with wrap baseline added","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fixture","gpu-harness","postswarm-2026-04-27"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-ombfl.4","depends_on_id":"ft-ombfl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:06:19.762519Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-ombfl.4","depends_on_id":"ft-ombfl.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:09:05.209659Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-ombfl.5","title":"[ft-ombfl][gpu-fixture] Unicode + CJK + emoji + fallback fixture (catches ft-6scm7-class shaping regressions)","description":"# GPU fixture — unicode + CJK + emoji + fallback

## Background
The ft-6scm7 bug closed during the 2026-04-27 session was a
shaping/clusters regression in font.shape() that corrupted RTL +
Indic acceptance. A golden harness covering CJK + emoji + complex
script would have caught that earlier. This fixture is the canary
for that bug class.

## Why this matters
- ft-6scm7 specifically: the shaping path was passing None for
  paragraph_range, breaking bidi/complex-script. The fix landed in
  357fb60c (test) + ab11211d (fix) but no GUI golden caught it
  pre-merge. This fixture closes that detection gap.
- More broadly: emoji fallback, CJK width handling, RTL bidi, and
  combining-mark stacking are all common regression sources in
  terminal renderers.

## Scope
Author 4 fixtures under tests/golden/gpu/:
1. **text-cjk-mixed/**: a paragraph mixing English ASCII, simplified
   Chinese, traditional Chinese, Japanese hiragana+katakana+kanji,
   Korean hangul. Each script on its own line for clean diffs.
2. **text-emoji-fallback/**: a line of common emoji including some
   that test fallback (regional indicators, ZWJ sequences, skin tone
   modifiers, flags). Test grapheme-cluster boundary correctness.
3. **text-rtl-arabic-hebrew/**: lines with Arabic shaping and
   Hebrew, including bidi mixing with English numerals.
4. **text-combining-marks/**: Latin-with-diacritics where combining
   marks should stack correctly above base glyphs.

## Out of scope
- Indic-script fixtures (separate bead if needed; ft-6scm7 mentioned
  Indic specifically — consider adding it here OR filing a follow-on).
- Vertical text (CJK vertical) — out of scope for terminal grid.

## Approach
1. Author input.json files for each fixture with carefully chosen
   strings (avoid characters that aren't in the pinned font set).
2. Specify font fallback chain explicitly in each fixture's meta.json
   (e.g., NotoSansCJK + NotoColorEmoji + ...) so the test result is
   deterministic and not machine-font-dependent.
3. Bundle the necessary fonts under tests/fixtures/fonts/ if they
   aren't already there.
4. Generate goldens via --update-goldens.
5. Manually verify each golden looks correct (no tofu boxes, no
   missing glyphs, correct combining stacks).

## Acceptance criteria
- All 4 fixtures committed with goldens.
- Each runs to PASS.
- Tofu (replacement glyph U+FFFD) does NOT appear in any golden —
  if it does, the fallback chain is misconfigured.
- The ft-6scm7 regression is detectable by mutating
  text-rtl-arabic-hebrew with an old (broken) shaping output —
  confirm the fixture fails.

## Testing
- Fixture self-test: each runs and passes.
- Cross-machine determinism: render on macOS + CI; comparator
  passes.
- Regression-detection: revert the ft-6scm7 fix locally; confirm
  text-rtl-arabic-hebrew now FAILS with a diff PNG. Then re-apply
  the fix.
- Logging: per-fixture render_ms recorded; CJK fixture should be
  within 2x of basic-text render_ms (sanity check on shaping cost).

## Risks / considerations
- Emoji rendering depends on color-emoji font; ensure
  NotoColorEmoji or equivalent is bundled.
- ZWJ-sequence support is renderer-specific. If our renderer
  doesn't fully support family-emoji ZWJ, the fixture should pin
  the rendered behavior even if it's not "correct" per Unicode —
  the harness catches CHANGES, not absolute correctness.
- Font licensing: bundle only OFL-licensed fonts under
  tests/fixtures/fonts/ and document the licenses.

## Labels
- postswarm-2026-04-27
- gpu-harness
- fixture

## References
- ft-6scm7 (the bug this catches).
- 357fb60c, ab11211d (the fix commits).
- GPU_RENDERER (renderer integration prerequisite).

 {"id":"ft-ombfl.6","title":"[ft-ombfl][gpu-fixture] Box-drawing characters fixture: borders, tables, half/quarter-block shading","description":"# GPU fixture — box-drawing characters

## Background
Terminal apps lean heavily on box-drawing characters (U+2500-257F)
and block elements (U+2580-259F) for borders, tables, progress bars,
and ASCII art. These characters have specific connection semantics
(line joins) and shading levels that are easy to break in renderer
refactors.

## Why this matters
- Half-block / quarter-block shading is critical for rendering
  pixelated images in terminal (used by ranger, lf, ueberzug-style
  previewers).
- Line-drawing connections must align across cell boundaries; a
  1px misalignment is visually obvious and breaks tables.
- ft already uses box-drawing in robot-mode output (table borders
  in `ft robot rules show`).

## Scope
Author 3 fixtures under tests/golden/gpu/:
1. **box-drawing-table/**: a 5x5 table using U+2500-257F joins.
   Verifies cells share line-pixels at boundaries.
2. **box-drawing-shaded-blocks/**: a gradient using
   U+2581-2588 (lower one-eighth to full block) and shading
   U+2591-2593 (light/medium/dark shade).
3. **box-drawing-rounded-corners/**: U+256D-2570 rounded box
   corners (modern Unicode boxes).

## Out of scope
- Powerline glyphs (private-use range, font-specific) — separate.
- Sextants / Teletext (less common).

## Approach
1. Author input.json with carefully chosen box-drawing sequences.
2. Generate goldens via --update-goldens.
3. Manually inspect: cell-boundary lines must connect pixel-perfect.
4. Commit.

## Acceptance criteria
- All 3 fixtures committed.
- Tests pass.
- Box-drawing connection lines are pixel-aligned at cell boundaries
  (visible in a zoomed inspection of golden.png).
- Block-element shading shows visible gradient (not banding).

## Testing
- Fixture self-test: each runs, passes.
- Mutation test: shift cell width by 1px; confirm fixture fails
  with a diff showing misaligned joins.
- Visual inspection: open golden.png at 4x zoom; confirm joins.

## Risks / considerations
- Box-drawing rendering depends on the font's metrics. Pin the
  font (probably the same as text-basic, e.g., Liberation Mono or
  a dedicated terminal font like JetBrains Mono).
- Cell width must be specified precisely in meta.json.

## Labels
- postswarm-2026-04-27
- gpu-harness
- fixture

## References
- GPU_RENDERER (prerequisite).
- ft-ombfl (parent).
- Unicode block: Box Drawing (U+2500-257F).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod_1","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:06:20.670940Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T04:50:53.915011Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fixture","gpu-harness","postswarm-2026-04-27"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-ombfl.6","depends_on_id":"ft-ombfl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:06:20.670940Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-ombfl.6","depends_on_id":"ft-ombfl.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:09:05.938517Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"ft-ombfl.7","title":"[ft-ombfl][gpu-fixture] Cursor states fixture: block / underline / beam, with/without focus, blink-disabled","description":"# GPU fixture — cursor states

## Background
Cursor rendering is one of the highest-visibility regression
surfaces. Block, underline, beam shapes; focused vs unfocused
appearance; blink behavior. Every change to the cursor shader risks
breaking one of these states.

## Why this matters
- Users rely on visual cursor cues for orientation. A bug here is
  immediately visible and frustrating.
- Cursor color, blending, and contrast (against arbitrary
  backgrounds) are subtle; goldens catch sneaky regressions.

## Scope
Author 6 fixtures under tests/golden/gpu/:
1. **cursor-block-focused/**: cursor in default block shape, pane
   focused, on a normal background.
2. **cursor-block-unfocused/**: same but pane unfocused (cursor
   typically becomes a hollow outline).
3. **cursor-underline-focused/**: underline cursor shape.
4. **cursor-beam-focused/**: vertical beam cursor.
5. **cursor-on-emoji/**: cursor positioned over a wide emoji glyph
   (verifies cursor still visually clear).
6. **cursor-on-cjk/**: cursor over a CJK fullwidth glyph (cursor
   should span the full cell width).

All fixtures: blink DISABLED (deterministic).

## Out of scope
- Blinking-cursor frame capture (would need timing — separate bead).
- Custom cursor colors via OSC sequences — could be a follow-on.

## Approach
1. Author 6 input.json files specifying cursor type + position +
   underlying cell.
2. Each meta.json specifies cursor_blink=false.
3. Generate goldens via --update-goldens.
4. Manually inspect: each cursor type visibly distinct.
5. Commit.

## Acceptance criteria
- All 6 fixtures committed.
- Tests pass.
- Each fixture's golden shows the cursor in the documented shape.
- cursor-on-emoji and cursor-on-cjk visibly span the full glyph
  cell.

## Testing
- Fixture self-test.
- Mutation: change cursor type from block to beam; confirm fixture
  fails.
- Mutation: change focus state; confirm focused vs unfocused
  fixtures both fail when focus value is wrong.

## Risks / considerations
- Cursor color often tracks the foreground/background palette. Pin
  the palette in meta.json so these tests aren't affected by theme.
- For cursor-on-emoji: the cursor should not OBSCURE the emoji
  but should be visible. Both states are valid renders depending on
  blend mode; pick one and pin it.

## Labels
- postswarm-2026-04-27
- gpu-harness
- fixture

## References
- GPU_RENDERER (prerequisite).
- ft-ombfl (parent).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod_2","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:06:21.123996Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T04:51:24.140862Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fixture","gpu-harness","postswarm-2026-04-27"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-ombfl.7","depends_on_id":"ft-ombfl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:06:21.123996Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-ombfl.7","depends_on_id":"ft-ombfl.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:09:06.307665Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ft-ombfl.8","title":"[ft-ombfl][gpu-fixture] Selection highlight + overlays fixture: char/word/line/rectangular selection, search hits, lens overlays","description":"# GPU fixture — selection highlight + overlays

## Background
Selection rendering and overlay layers are blend-heavy — easy to
break in renderer changes that touch alpha or layering order.

## Why this matters
- Selection is the most-frequent user interaction with cursor-on-text.
- Search hit highlights, error squiggles, and floating panel
  overlays all use the same blending substrate.

## Scope
Author 5 fixtures under tests/golden/gpu/:
1. **selection-char/**: a multi-line character selection.
2. **selection-word/**: word-mode selection (double-click style).
3. **selection-line/**: line-mode selection (triple-click).
4. **selection-rectangular/**: block selection across columns.
5. **overlay-search-hits/**: a search query with multiple highlighted
   hits in different colors (e.g., current hit + other matches).

## Out of scope
- Animated selection extension (drag preview) — out of scope for
  static fixtures.
- Multi-cursor selection (separate bead if a feature later).

## Approach
1. Author 5 input.json files specifying selection bounds + style.
2. Each fixture with deterministic palette (foreground/background +
   selection-bg color pinned).
3. Generate goldens.
4. Manually inspect: selection bg covers exactly the selected
   cells, no spillover, text remains readable.
5. Commit.

## Acceptance criteria
- All 5 fixtures committed.
- Tests pass.
- Selection bounds are pixel-correct.
- Text contrast against selection bg passes a manual readability
  check (no white-on-white).

## Testing
- Fixture self-test.
- Mutation: shift selection by 1 char; confirm fixture fails with
  diff showing edge-cell change.
- Mutation: change selection alpha; confirm fixture fails.

## Risks / considerations
- Selection rendering may differ between platforms (macOS uses
  blend-mode multiply by convention; Linux software-render may
  approximate). Pin the blend mode in meta.json or use a single
  CI runner.
- Rectangular selection is rarer; document its semantics in the
  fixture's input.json.

## Labels
- postswarm-2026-04-27
- gpu-harness
- fixture

## References
- GPU_RENDERER (prerequisite).

-{"id":"ft-ombfl.9","title":"[ft-ombfl][gpu-fixture] Multi-pane layout fixture: split divider geometry + focused/unfocused borders + 3+ panes","description":"# GPU fixture — multi-pane layout

## Background
ft-1memj.28 closed the divider-geometry test (bf9db5d5) — that test
pinned a single split's geometry. This fixture goes broader:
multi-pane layouts (2/3/4+ panes), focused vs unfocused border
treatment, and the cell-padding around dividers.

## Why this matters
- frankenterm is a swarm-native terminal — multi-pane layouts are
  the dominant use case (the 2026-04-27 swarm itself ran with 7
  panes). Regressions in multi-pane render are core breakage.
- Divider rendering is shared across split, tab, and floating
  surfaces — a fix in one can break the others.

## Scope
Author 5 fixtures under tests/golden/gpu/:
1. **multipane-2split-h/**: horizontal split, 2 panes side-by-side,
   left focused.
2. **multipane-2split-v/**: vertical split, 2 panes stacked, top
   focused.
3. **multipane-grid-4/**: 2x2 grid of panes, top-left focused.
4. **multipane-deep-nested/**: 5-pane layout with mixed splits
   (test divider corners and T-joins).
5. **multipane-floating-overlay/**: a floating pane on top of a
   normal split layout.

All fixtures: cursor blink off, deterministic content per pane.

## Out of scope
- Resize animation (static frames only).
- Tab bar rendering (separate fixture if needed).

## Approach
1. Author input.json for each, with explicit pane tree + focus state.
2. meta.json specifies viewport size, dpi, divider color.
3. Generate goldens.
4. Manual inspection: divider lines connect at corners, focused
   border is visibly distinct, padding is consistent.
5. Commit.

## Acceptance criteria
- All 5 fixtures committed.
- Tests pass.
- Divider corners are pixel-aligned (T-joins, +-joins, L-joins).
- Focused vs unfocused border is visibly distinct in goldens.
- Floating overlay correctly shadows underlying panes if shadow
  is enabled.

## Testing
- Fixture self-test.
- Mutation: change focus from one pane to another; confirm fixture
  fails with diff showing border swap.
- Mutation: shift one pane by 1 cell; confirm divider misalignment
  catches.
- Logging: per-fixture render_ms; multi-pane should scale roughly
  linearly with pane count (sanity check; not a perf gate).

## Risks / considerations
- Float-overlay shadow rendering may include per-frame randomness
  (e.g., dithering). Pin a deterministic shadow algorithm or
  disable shadows in meta.json.
- Deep-nested layout's divider intersections are the trickiest;
  triple-check corner pixel by manual inspection before committing.

## Labels
- postswarm-2026-04-27
- gpu-harness
- fixture

## References
- ft-1memj.28 / bf9db5d5 (divider single-split geometry).
- GPU_RENDERER (prerequisite).
- ft-ombfl (parent).","notes":"## Polish pass round 3 (concrete resize sequence)

### multipane-resize-static-snapshot fixture sequence
The previous polish added this fixture but didn't specify the
resize sequence. Concrete values:
- Initial viewport: 80x24 (standard terminal).
- Step 1: 100x30 (wider + taller).
- Step 2: 120x40 (continue scaling).
- Step 3: 90x35 (shrink width).
- Step 4 (final, captured): 100x35.
Each step exercises a different resize concern:
- 80→100x24→30: pure growth, atlas may need to add new lines.
- 100→120x30→40: aggressive growth, glyph cache may evict.
- 120→90x40→35: width shrink, must re-flow content.
- 90→100x35: final stable state, captured as golden.

Layout: 2x2 grid of panes throughout the resize. Each pane has
deterministic content: pane 0 = 5-line ASCII, pane 1 = 3-line
CJK, pane 2 = box-drawing table, pane 3 = colored selection.
This way the resize exercises ALL render paths (text, CJK,
box-drawing, selection).

### Per-pane render_ms in fixture metadata
Update fixture's expected.json schema to record per-pane
render_ms in addition to whole-frame:
```json
{
  "fixture_name": "multipane-resize-static-snapshot",
  "panes": [
    {"id": 0, "expected_render_ms_max": 5},
    {"id": 1, "expected_render_ms_max": 8},
    ...
  ]
}
```
Catches single-pane regressions hidden in whole-frame averages.

### Cursor blink off in ALL panes (re-emphasized)
Confirmed for all 6 fixtures in this bead. Add a meta.json
field `cursor_blink_disabled_globally: true` and reject any
fixture missing it (loader-level check).

### Total fixtures in this bead = 6 (was 5+1)

+{"id":"ft-ombfl.9","title":"[ft-ombfl][gpu-fixture] Multi-pane layout fixture: split divider geometry + focused/unfocused borders + 3+ panes","description":"# GPU fixture — multi-pane layout

## Background
ft-1memj.28 closed the divider-geometry test (bf9db5d5) — that test
pinned a single split's geometry. This fixture goes broader:
multi-pane layouts (2/3/4+ panes), focused vs unfocused border
treatment, and the cell-padding around dividers.

## Why this matters
- frankenterm is a swarm-native terminal — multi-pane layouts are
  the dominant use case (the 2026-04-27 swarm itself ran with 7
  panes). Regressions in multi-pane render are core breakage.
- Divider rendering is shared across split, tab, and floating
  surfaces — a fix in one can break the others.

## Scope
Author 5 fixtures under tests/golden/gpu/:
1. **multipane-2split-h/**: horizontal split, 2 panes side-by-side,
   left focused.
2. **multipane-2split-v/**: vertical split, 2 panes stacked, top
   focused.
3. **multipane-grid-4/**: 2x2 grid of panes, top-left focused.
4. **multipane-deep-nested/**: 5-pane layout with mixed splits
   (test divider corners and T-joins).
5. **multipane-floating-overlay/**: a floating pane on top of a
   normal split layout.

All fixtures: cursor blink off, deterministic content per pane.

## Out of scope
- Resize animation (static frames only).
- Tab bar rendering (separate fixture if needed).

## Approach
1. Author input.json for each, with explicit pane tree + focus state.
2. meta.json specifies viewport size, dpi, divider color.
3. Generate goldens.
4. Manual inspection: divider lines connect at corners, focused
   border is visibly distinct, padding is consistent.
5. Commit.

## Acceptance criteria
- All 5 fixtures committed.
- Tests pass.
- Divider corners are pixel-aligned (T-joins, +-joins, L-joins).
- Focused vs unfocused border is visibly distinct in goldens.
- Floating overlay correctly shadows underlying panes if shadow
  is enabled.

## Testing
- Fixture self-test.
- Mutation: change focus from one pane to another; confirm fixture
  fails with diff showing border swap.
- Mutation: shift one pane by 1 cell; confirm divider misalignment
  catches.
- Logging: per-fixture render_ms; multi-pane should scale roughly
  linearly with pane count (sanity check; not a perf gate).

## Risks / considerations
- Float-overlay shadow rendering may include per-frame randomness
  (e.g., dithering). Pin a deterministic shadow algorithm or
  disable shadows in meta.json.
- Deep-nested layout's divider intersections are the trickiest;
  triple-check corner pixel by manual inspection before committing.

## Labels
- postswarm-2026-04-27
- gpu-harness
- fixture

## References
- ft-1memj.28 / bf9db5d5 (divider single-split geometry).
- GPU_RENDERER (prerequisite).
- ft-ombfl (parent).","notes":"## Polish pass round 3 (concrete resize sequence)

### multipane-resize-static-snapshot fixture sequence
The previous polish added this fixture but didn't specify the
resize sequence. Concrete values:
- Initial viewport: 80x24 (standard terminal).
- Step 1: 100x30 (wider + taller).
- Step 2: 120x40 (continue scaling).
- Step 3: 90x35 (shrink width).
- Step 4 (final, captured): 100x35.
Each step exercises a different resize concern:
- 80→100x24→30: pure growth, atlas may need to add new lines.
- 100→120x30→40: aggressive growth, glyph cache may evict.
- 120→90x40→35: width shrink, must re-flow content.
- 90→100x35: final stable state, captured as golden.

Layout: 2x2 grid of panes throughout the resize. Each pane has
deterministic content: pane 0 = 5-line ASCII, pane 1 = 3-line
CJK, pane 2 = box-drawing table, pane 3 = colored selection.
This way the resize exercises ALL render paths (text, CJK,
box-drawing, selection).

### Per-pane render_ms in fixture metadata
Update fixture's expected.json schema to record per-pane
render_ms in addition to whole-frame:
```json
{
  "fixture_name": "multipane-resize-static-snapshot",
  "panes": [
    {"id": 0, "expected_render_ms_max": 5},
    {"id": 1, "expected_render_ms_max": 8},
    ...
  ]
}
```
Catches single-pane regressions hidden in whole-frame averages.

### Cursor blink off in ALL panes (re-emphasized)
Confirmed for all 6 fixtures in this bead. Add a meta.json
field `cursor_blink_disabled_globally: true` and reject any
fixture missing it (loader-level check).

### Total fixtures in this bead = 6 (was 5+1)

 {"id":"ft-p2bjm","title":"[fuzz] add replay::Recording::from_bytes fuzz target for .war recording binary format","description":"Surface: crates/frankenterm-core/src/replay.rs:128 — pub fn Recording::from_bytes(data: &[u8]) and the internal parse_frame at :36. This is the binary .war recording file loader. Attack surface: any command that opens a user-supplied recording path (ft replay load, session restore, CI-fed fixtures). Oversize cap at 256 MiB was added in 3a0cac70 (security) but no coverage-guided harness exercises the parse_frame byte machinery.

Proposed harness: fuzz/fuzz_targets/replay_recording_from_bytes.rs
  #![no_main]
  use libfuzzer_sys::fuzz_target;
  use frankenterm_core::replay::Recording;
  fuzz_target!(|data: &[u8]| {
      if data.len() > 8_000_000 { return; }
      let _ = Recording::from_bytes(data);
  });

Corpus seeds: fuzz/corpus/replay_recording_from_bytes/ — (a) empty, (b) single Output frame with 1-byte payload, (c) multi-frame recording with interleaved OSC133 markers + PaneOutput from an existing war fixture, (d) frame claiming huge payload_len (boundary-of-bounds), (e) truncated frame header mid-read, (f) zero-duration recording.

Oracle: no panic, no allocation explosion, no infinite loop (tracked by libfuzzer timeout). Structure-aware Arbitrary impl on RecordingFrame could accelerate discovery after initial coverage plateau.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-23T05:49:32.230576Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:29:40.334910Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:29:40.334105Z","close_reason":"Already landed in 94f171e7; replay_recording_from_bytes is present in fuzz/Cargo.toml and cargo fuzz list.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
